title: Floral Filipino Gelatin Bao
description: A unique Filipino dish featuring unflavored gelatin, perfect for festive occasions. This recipe is a refreshing twist on traditional Filipino cuisine, and is low in carbs making it a great option for those on a diet.

ingredients.name: Unflavored Gelatin
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use unflavored gelatin to maintain the natural flavors of the dish.

ingredients.name: Ube Extract
ingredients.notes: Add a hint of ube fragrance to the gelatin.

ingredients.name: Violet Petals
ingredients.notes: Use edible violet petals to give the gelatin a floral flavor.

ingredients.name: Red Bean Paste
ingredients.notes: Add a pop of color and a sweet flavor to the gelatin.

ingredients.name: Coconut Milk
ingredients.notes: Enhance the creaminess of the dish with coconut milk.

ingredients.name: Pandan Extract
ingredients.notes: Use pandan extract to give the gelatin a distinct Filipino flavor.

ingredients.name: Ginger Juice
ingredients.notes: Add a hint of spice to the gelatin.

instructions: Combine unflavored gelatin and water in a small saucepan and whisk to dissolve. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the gelatin has fully dissolved.
Add ube extract, violet petals, red bean paste, coconut milk, salt, pandan extract, and ginger juice to the gelatin mixture. Stir to combine.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature.
Pour the gelatin mixture into individual molds or a large baking dish. Refrigerate until set, about 3 hours.
Once set, unmold the gelatin or cut it into bite-sized pieces. Serve chilled and enjoy!
